name: bespunky-workflow
description: BeSpunky ways-of-working: the process, order, and methodology of how work moves from idea to production — independent of what's being built. branch-and-release: the house git methodology — a one-way promotion pipeline across four branches (feature → development → staging → main, each a strict ancestor of the next so promotions are clean fast-forwards), unrelated work isolated in per-feature git worktrees off the integration branch (with the Nx-workspace-root override that stops tooling silently targeting the main tree), the relevance check that runs before touching any file, rebase-and-re-verify at the one divergence point (feature → development) rather than resolving conflicts on the shared branch, and three human-gated promotions (feature done → development; go to staging; go live → main). Deploy bindings (which branches auto-deploy to which environment) are a per-project parameter stated in the project's CLAUDE.md; this skill carries the durable method. Serving an in-flight worktree without merging it back is provided by the unified serve target in BeSpunky-scaffolded (Nx) projects (`nx serve <app> --worktree=<branch|slug>`, with automatic per-worktree port isolation and a pretty <slug>.localhost domain). local-server-isolation: when launching a local server to test a change, bind a RANDOM free port (never the default/forwarded port, which belongs to a server the user launched manually) — you test headless so you never need the forwarded ports; and never reap the user's running Firebase emulator suite (reuse it, or run your own on a shifted port set). feature-package: a feature is a PACKAGE, not a scatter of files — one effort, one slug, one folder. The effort's slug (the one branch-and-release already picks for the branch feat/<slug> and worktree) is its IDENTITY, and its package is docs/features/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>/ — born WITH the worktree, filled as the work happens, never written up at the end (a doc written afterwards is a memory, and memories are where the reasons go missing). Every skill that produces something durable writes INTO it instead of inventing a private home: the design quartet's BRIEF.md / VISION.md / STAGING.md / DECISION.md (distill-the-brief → envision-the-experience → stage-the-vision → mock-to-choose), the throwaway self-ignoring mocks/, and the effort's handoffs/ batons (session-handoff, located on resume by computing the newest package for the branch's slug — no committed pointer file, which would conflict on every promotion). Two rules keep it honest — the CONCLUSION is durable, the EVIDENCE is disposable (decisions and roads-not-taken are committed and permanent; mocks/scratch/spikes are self-ignoring, nothing may depend on them, and they're binned by default), and the user's OWN WORDS are the most valuable line in the package (quote the sentence that settled it; never paraphrase). The date prefix makes the trail chronological and lets a later pass at the same feature sit beside the first instead of overwriting it. Not a documentation ritual and not a required set of files — an effort writes only what it actually produced. It answers: six months on, why was it done this way, and what did we already rule out? session-handoff: carry a live effort across a context boundary into a FRESH session — one skill, two modes (capture writes the relay baton, resume picks it up). The baton is a distillation (goal/intent, current state, immediately-actionable next step, decisions and roads-not-taken, insights, constraints, verification state, anchors) — never a transcript dump — committed as timestamped docs/handoffs/<ts>.md with a latest.md pointer. First-class: every CORRECTION the user made to Claude (behavioral, conceptual, code, process), routed by scope — durable corrections promoted to the persistent memory store so a lesson is never re-learned, task-local ones kept in the baton. Resume is a re-grounding (verify anchors, report drift, confirm the next step), not blind trust. Complements auto-compaction and the memory store rather than replacing them. project-standing: the COLD pick-up — orient in a project you've been away from. DERIVES where things stand from git + the feature packages (never a hand-maintained status doc, which is the first thing to rot): which efforts are LIVE, STALLED, or CONCLUDED, which handoff baton to read first, what was in flight when everyone walked away — live efforts rendered in full, concluded ones collapsed to a one-line conclusion (read from DECISION.md status: frontmatter) so orientation costs the same at effort #300 as at #3. Three scopes — orient (default, live tier only), history (search the archive tier ON DEMAND for 'have we tried this before'), and archive-sweep (git mv aged-concluded efforts into docs/features/archive/<year>/ — additive, consented, never an erase). Distinct from session-handoff: the COLD front door that DISCOVERS forgotten efforts, versus the HOT single-effort relay that CONTINUES a known one. Made reliable — not hoped-for — by two hooks: a SessionStart hook that stays silent unless in-flight work has gone dormant past a threshold, then RELAYS a fact pointing at the skill (detect-don't-execute, like project-starter's); and a PreCompact hook that writes a mechanical checkpoint (branch, HEAD, uncommitted files — the perishable half) into the live effort's package before context is summarized away, even headless, then asks the model to distill it into a proper baton. Lifecycle: append while live, distill at the merge/abandon boundary (the closing conclusion), archive never erase — never rewrite a past record to look tidy.
